meta.description: Does having a group name/identity change engagement? Named crew with motto vs anonymous room code vs named crew with full season standings. Tests whether persistent group identity creates stronger attachment and competitive investment.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Alright, let me see Pat's hands... Yeah, that checks out. Legit.
marcus.internal_thought: He's been doing it, fine, whatever — but I need to focus on the actual game setup before things get too chaotic.

jade.dialogue: Wait wait wait — Pat, did you actually pull this off?? I need to see your hands, show me RIGHT NOW. This is peak chaos energy and I'm here for it!
jade.internal_thought: Oh my god, the night is STARTING and Pat's already making a move — this is exactly what I came for, let's GO.

pat.dialogue: Wait, hold up—did I actually just pull this off? I've been nursing this drink with my right hand like an idiot for the last minute. Let's see if anyone caught it.
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect—caught myself mid-slip, everyone's watching now, and I get to see if my poker face holds up during the vote; also love that the AI picked a mission that forces me to stay engaged without derailing the whole night.

river.dialogue: Yeah, looks legit. Left hand only would've been obvious if he broke it.
river.internal_thought: Pat probably got caught using his right hand like five seconds in and is just claiming anyway, but I'm not going to be the one to fight it this early.

timeline.title: Toast Master
timeline.description: First person to raise their glass and give a toast (real or ridiculous) wins.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

pat.dialogue: Oh, perfect timing—everyone grab a glass! Who's going first? This is peak chaos energy right now.
pat.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of low-stakes, inclusive moment that keeps the whole table engaged—Jade's already loving it, Marcus is still finding his rhythm, and a toast gets everyone in the same headspace at once.

river.dialogue: I'll pass on this one—too much performance energy required right now.
river.internal_thought: Toast master is too obvious a grab, and I'm not about to be the person scrambling for attention 5 minutes in when I haven't even settled into the vibe yet.

alex.dialogue: Oh COME ON, a toast? That's literally free chaos — I'm going first and it's gonna be unhinged. Watch this.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of stupid simple mission that either becomes legendary or falls flat, and I'm about to make it legendary.

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Pat's claiming Compliment Chain? I mean... sure, I guess I saw some compliments happening. Who's judging?
marcus.internal_thought: We're still in setup and this is the third interruption in six minutes—this is exactly the kind of chaos creep that kills focus, and Pat's basically guaranteed to get this one anyway since Jade and Alex are in love-it mode.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD YES, Pat absolutely crushed that Left Hand mission — I was WATCHING. Okay okay, we need a tribunal like RIGHT NOW, who's judging? I'm volunteering, I saw everything, let's gooo!
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly what I came for — Pat's actually killing it tonight and I get to be part of validating it, this energy is PERFECT.

tyler.dialogue: Oh yeah, Pat got people to compliment each other? That's... I mean, I didn't see it happen, but sure, sounds legit. Do we need judges for that?
tyler.internal_thought: Pat's up 20 points already? Damn. Wait, did that thing actually happen or is Pat just claiming it? Honestly I was scrolling like 30 seconds ago. The crew thing is kind of corny but also I don't want to be the guy who tanks us this season.

pat.dialogue: Oh damn, did I actually pull that off? Wait—we need judges. Who's feeling confident about whether that actually happened? Jade, Alex, you two seem sharp tonight.
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect timing—I've got everyone locked in, the energy is climbing, and now I'm actually in the spotlight, which means I need to be gracious about it and immediately shift focus to getting other people involved so it doesn't feel like I'm hogging points.

river.dialogue: Pat definitely got people saying nice things about each other. I wasn't really tracking it, but... yeah, that happened.
river.internal_thought: Pat's been riding high all night and now they're fishing for a judge? I could volunteer but I'd rather not draw attention. Let someone else run the tribunal.

votingMechanic.name: Hot Seat
votingMechanic.description: 3 rapid-fire questions. Answer them all in 10 seconds or fail.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Alright Pat, let's see if you actually earned this one. Lay it on me.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's been on a roll tonight and honestly the energy's been solid, so I'll give them the benefit of the doubt—but I'm watching for BS.

jade.dialogue: OH PAT, STOP IT RIGHT NOW — you're gonna steamroll us all! Okay okay, hot seat, Pat's in the hot seat, let's go, ask them EVERYTHING, I need to see if this is legit!
jade.internal_thought: Pat is absolutely ON FIRE tonight and honestly I'm just thrilled to watch someone go off like this — also I'm definitely catching them on something in the next mission, mark my words.

pat.dialogue: Oh hell yeah, I'm taking this one! Three questions, ten seconds—let's GO. I'm feeling it tonight.
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect timing—I'm already riding high, the energy in the room is *exactly* where I want it, and claiming a mission myself actually models the vibe for everyone else to stay aggressive and confident.

river.dialogue: Oh, Pat's actually got this one locked. Three questions in ten seconds? Yeah, he's been running the room all night—he'll nail it.
river.internal_thought: Pat's already at 50 and hasn't even slowed down; he's definitely ready for this. I should vote yes and stop wasting mental energy on it.

alex.dialogue: Oh shit, Pat's going for it — okay okay, three questions, ten seconds, let's see if you actually pulled this off or if you're just riding the vibe.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's claiming Laugh Riot with a hot seat? This is EXACTLY the kind of escalation I live for — either Pat gets exposed or we all get to watch them sweat under pressure, win-win.

votingMechanic.name: The Auction
votingMechanic.description: Bid your own points. Highest bidder decides the outcome.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Okay, hold up — Pat, did you actually nail five minutes without laughing, or are we doing this the hard way? Because I'm about to bid on this.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's on a run tonight and I need to know if this claim is legit before I waste points challenging it — but honestly, the AI targeting me with 'No Laughing Zone' right after I was half-paying attention feels weirdly personal, and I don't like that.

jade.dialogue: OH HELL YES, Pat's going for it!! Okay okay, we need to put REAL money on this — Pat, I'm bidding like five points you held that, because I WATCHED you and you were SOLID. Who's calling cap? Let's GO!
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY why we're here — Pat's already on a tear, the energy is PEAKED, and I get to be the chaos referee deciding who wins? Undefeated night, sign me up for more of this.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat did the no-laugh thing? Like right now? I didn't even see that happen, man.
tyler.internal_thought: Did Pat actually pull off not laughing for five minutes or is he just claiming it? I wasn't watching. Also my phone just buzzed and I'm pretty sure that's my fantasy league trade alert.

pat.dialogue: Oh, I'm claiming this one—been stone-faced all night waiting for my moment. But hold up, we're doing the Auction? That's spicy. Who's brave enough to bid against me?
pat.internal_thought: Perfect timing—the vibe is exactly right for this, everyone's already laughing, and I can use this to keep the energy hot without burning out the night.

river.dialogue: Pat's been locked in all night. I'd say he probably held it together—but honestly, with everyone else going hard, that's the real test.
river.internal_thought: Pat's hyper right now and everyone's feeding into it; if anyone could actually hold a straight face through that chaos, it'd be him, but the AI definitely timed this to catch him when people are laughing at everything.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

marcus.dialogue: Okay, hold up — did Jade actually steer us off topic or are we voting on vibes here? I need specifics before I'm calling this legit.
marcus.internal_thought: She's been loud all night and everyone's hyped, so yeah, probably happened, but I'm not giving away 10 points on a guess — let me actually think about what we were talking about.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD, DID I ACTUALLY PULL THAT OFF?! Wait, wait — okay, okay, I need to think about this for like two seconds because I genuinely don't remember if I steered us or if we just naturally went there, but like... I THINK I did? Either way, let's VOTE, let's GO, this is so fun!
jade.internal_thought: I'm claiming this because I lowkey KNOW I steered that conversation but also I'm riding so high right now that even if it's bullshit I just want to see what happens — everyone's so into it tonight, this is exactly why I came.

tyler.dialogue: Jade, wait—did you actually like... steer us to a different topic? I feel like I was looking at my phone, but yeah, I guess that counts. Going with legit.
tyler.internal_thought: She totally did hijack that conversation about whatever, but I zoned out so I can't even remember what the original topic was—I'm just gonna vote yes because everyone's vibing and Jade's having fun with it.

pat.dialogue: Okay, okay—Jade, that was SMOOTH. I saw you steer us from the Marcus-Tyler card thing straight into that whole rant about bad coffee. Legit claim, everyone vote it up.
pat.internal_thought: She absolutely nailed that hijack—came out of nowhere, got everyone talking, and nobody even clocked it until now. This is the kind of move I live for, and it keeps Jade in the hunt when she's been sitting at zero. Perfect pacing, perfect moment.

river.dialogue: Yeah, I saw that coming. Jade's been steering us all night—she definitely got the topic shift in there.
river.internal_thought: Jade's been loud and energetic enough that she probably did hijack a topic; I wasn't paying close enough attention to catch exactly when, but voting legit seems safe and she deserves the point.

votingMechanic.name: The Alibi
votingMechanic.description: Claimant and a random witness both tell the story. Do they match?

marcus.dialogue: Alright, Jade. The Alibi test—let's see if you actually kept that face locked down or if you cracked like you always do when Pat gets going. Pick your witness.
marcus.internal_thought: She's 20 points ahead and I'm stuck at zero; I need to either call bullshit on her claim or actually watch her prove it so I'm not flying blind when I make my own move.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD YES, okay okay, I'm claiming this RIGHT NOW — I have been LOCKED IN, absolutely stone-faced this whole game, ask anyone, I've been a STATUE, let's DO this!
jade.internal_thought: This is literally perfect timing — the AI KNOWS I live for this stuff and it's hitting exactly right, I'm about to absolutely nail this, watch me.

tyler.dialogue: Oh dude, Jade's going for it—okay, okay, I'm watching this time. Someone get a witness, let's see if she actually kept it together.
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, this is actually gonna be funny because Jade laughs at EVERYTHING, so either she's lying or she's some kind of stone-faced demon—also ngl the AI picking this exact mission feels lowkey creepy but also kinda sick that it knows we crack up so easy.

pat.dialogue: Oh, Jade's going for Straight Face? Let's see—who was with you when this happened? We need a witness to lock this down. This is the good stuff.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's on fire tonight and I love it; this claim is legit spicy because everyone's been laughing constantly, so if she actually kept her face straight that's impressive—gotta get a fair witness on this one to keep the vote clean.

river.dialogue: Oh, this is good. Jade's been playing hard all night—I actually watched her face during that last round. Witness me.
river.internal_thought: Jade's been the one cracking jokes and steering everything, so if anyone's been *keeping* a straight face, it'd be interesting—but I saw her grin twice when Marcus fumbled that rule question, so this might be the tell.

alex.dialogue: Oh this is PERFECT — Jade's about to either nail this or implode trying, and I get a front-row seat. I'm absolutely watching this one.
alex.internal_thought: Jade's riding high at 20 points, Pat's untouchable at 90, and this 'Alibi' mechanic is basically asking me to catch someone in a lie — this is exactly the kind of theater I showed up for.

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: OKAY PAT, PAT PAT PAT — you're gonna have to SELL me on this one because honestly I was so locked in I might've missed it, but like, YES, let's hear it, let's GO!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been CLEANING UP tonight and honestly it's insane to watch, but did they actually catch someone breaking a rule or are they just being confident? Either way I'm hyped.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat—hold up. What rule? Like, what did you actually catch? I was in the zone right now, man.
tyler.internal_thought: I was finally locked in on the actual game and now I gotta pivot to figure out what Pat's claiming he saw, which I definitely missed because I was focused on the round.

pat.dialogue: Oh, I'm claiming this one—caught Tyler slipping on the card draw rules like two rounds ago. Fifteen seconds? I don't even need that, let me just lock it in.
pat.internal_thought: Tyler broke the 'no looking ahead' rule when he was setting up earlier and I've been waiting for the right moment to cash this in—plus it keeps me from coasting and reminds everyone the host is actually playing too.

river.dialogue: Wait, hold on—what rule did you actually catch? Because if you're pulling something I missed, I'm genuinely interested to hear it.
river.internal_thought: Pat's been playing sharp all night, but I've been watching too—he better have actually seen something or this vote gets interesting.

alex.dialogue: Oh WAIT, Pat — okay, you're claiming Rule Lawyer? I need to see this because I've been watching like a hawk and I'm pretty sure someone's been playing fast and loose with the score tracker. Lay it on me, fifteen seconds, let's go.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's at 90 points and riding high, so either they actually caught something legit or this is a beautiful bluff I'm about to call — either way, this is the moment I've been waiting for.

votingMechanic.name: The Bribe
votingMechanic.description: The claimant can offer their own points to buy your silence.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Wait—Pat, you're saying YOU figured out who did the Chaos Detector? That's... actually smart. Hold up, how much you offering me to shut up about it?
tyler.internal_thought: Okay, this is actually legit clever and Pat's been crushing it tonight, but also I was literally just paying attention so I actually remember who did what—why is everyone suddenly good at this game?

pat.dialogue: Wait, hold up—I'm claiming Chaos Detector? That's ballsy. Okay, who do you think I caught? And before you answer, what's the bribe looking like here?
pat.internal_thought: I actually DID catch someone mid-mission earlier, but I'm way up in points and this is a perfect moment to use The Bribe mechanic to either lock it in cheap or let someone else claim it—gotta keep the scores tight so the ending lands HARD.

river.dialogue: Pat's been moving so clean all night—yeah, I watched him slip that one in during the Tyler thing. But what's the bribe looking like?
river.internal_thought: Okay, this is the moment where River actually sits up a little—Pat's been genuinely impressive, and this claim is legit, and the fact that the AI customized this whole night around us... it actually feels kind of cool, not creepy.

alex.dialogue: Oh THIS is nasty — Pat's claiming Chaos Detector? Okay, I'm listening. What's the bribe? Because if you're trying to buy my vote right now, I'm gonna need it to be juicy enough to make me regret calling bullshit.
alex.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the moment I live for — Pat's at 100 points, sitting pretty, and now they're trying to slippery-slope me into silence with a bribe? I'm gonna vote however creates the most beautiful disaster, and watching Pat sweat this one out is already delicious.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 10/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

highlights.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
highlights.dialogue: Alright Pat, let's see if you actually earned this one. Lay it on me.

highlights.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
highlights.dialogue: Okay, hold up — Pat, did you actually nail five minutes without laughing, or are we doing this the hard way? Because I'm about to bid on this.

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: Okay, hold up — did Jade actually steer us off topic or are we voting on vibes here? I need specifics before I'm calling this legit.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Compliment Chain
lowPoints.dialogue: Pat's claiming Compliment Chain? I mean... sure, I guess I saw some compliments happening. Who's judging?

lowPoints.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, hold up — Pat, did you actually nail five minutes without laughing, or are we doing this the hard way? Because I'm about to bid on this.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Straight Face
lowPoints.dialogue: Alright, Jade. The Alibi test—let's see if you actually kept that face locked down or if you cracked like you always do when Pat gets going. Pick your witness.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Too many subjective claim validations that turned into debates instead of letting clean strategy play out.
assessment.best_moment: Calling out Pat on the No Laughing Zone claim—that's the kind of verification tension that actually sharpens the game.
assessment.narrative_summary: Went in studying the meta, came out third place on a party game I should've dominated. The claims system kept interrupting clean strategy moments with fuzzy judging calls. Pat was getting away with soft interpretations while I had to fight for every validation. Decent crew energy, but the game mechanics let too much subjectivity slip through—and that cost me.
suggestions: Establish objective criteria for claim validation before the round starts, not during.
Cap debate time on disputed claims—30 seconds max, then vote or move on.
Add a 'challenge fee' system where bad faith claims cost points, not just reputation.
Reduce event frequency during active strategy phases; save chaos for downtime.
Let the player with the claim defend it first before group pile-on—creates structure.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat claiming five minutes no laughing and then immediately cracking when Jade did the Straight Face test—the irony was perfect, even if it took forever to verify.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Claim: Compliment Chain
highlights.dialogue: OH MY GOD YES, Pat absolutely crushed that Left Hand mission — I was WATCHING. Okay okay, we need a tribunal like RIGHT NOW, who's judging? I'm volunteering, I saw everything, let's gooo!

highlights.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
highlights.dialogue: OH PAT, STOP IT RIGHT NOW — you're gonna steamroll us all! Okay okay, hot seat, Pat's in the hot seat, let's go, ask them EVERYTHING, I need to see if this is legit!

highlights.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
highlights.dialogue: OH HELL YES, Pat's going for it!! Okay okay, we need to put REAL money on this — Pat, I'm bidding like five points you held that, because I WATCHED you and you were SOLID. Who's calling cap? Let's GO!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
lowPoints.dialogue: OKAY PAT, PAT PAT PAT — you're gonna have to SELL me on this one because honestly I was so locked in I might've missed it, but like, YES, let's hear it, let's GO!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Left Hand Only
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait wait wait — Pat, did you actually pull this off?? I need to see your hands, show me RIGHT NOW. This is peak chaos energy and I'm here for it!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Compliment Chain
lowPoints.dialogue: OH MY GOD YES, Pat absolutely crushed that Left Hand mission — I was WATCHING. Okay okay, we need a tribunal like RIGHT NOW, who's judging? I'm volunteering, I saw everything, let's gooo!

assessment.biggest_complaint: I honestly can't think of one — Pat was on FIRE and everyone showed up ready to have fun.
assessment.best_moment: When Pat pulled off that Left Hand Only mission and I got to watch the whole thing unfold and then be the hype person about it — that's what it's all about.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was one of those nights where the chaos just CLICKED and everyone was locked in the same energy. Pat was absolutely unstoppable with the challenges, and I got to spend two hours doing what I love most — cheering people on, diving into every event, and watching this group just demolish each other with jokes. I finished second but honestly I was too busy hyping everyone else to care about my own score.
suggestions: Keep Pat off the Left Hand missions, that person is a threat to society
Maybe bring back some of the old seasonal callbacks — those always hit different when Marcus brings them up
More events where we can bet points on watching other people — the tribunal stuff was peak entertainment
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat attempted the Left Hand Only challenge and absolutely nailed it, then I went completely unhinged trying to convince everyone they witnessed greatness, and we ended up creating a whole tribunal to verify the footage — people were CRYING laughing at how serious I was about defending Pat's honor.

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Claim: Straight Face
highlights.dialogue: Oh dude, Jade's going for it—okay, okay, I'm watching this time. Someone get a witness, let's see if she actually kept it together.

highlights.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
highlights.dialogue: Wait—Pat, you're saying YOU figured out who did the Chaos Detector? That's... actually smart. Hold up, how much you offering me to shut up about it?

highlights.title: Claim: Compliment Chain
highlights.dialogue: Oh yeah, Pat got people to compliment each other? That's... I mean, I didn't see it happen, but sure, sounds legit. Do we need judges for that?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat—hold up. What rule? Like, what did you actually catch? I was in the zone right now, man.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
lowPoints.dialogue: Jade, wait—did you actually like... steer us to a different topic? I feel like I was looking at my phone, but yeah, I guess that counts. Going with legit.

lowPoints.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat did the no-laugh thing? Like right now? I didn't even see that happen, man.

assessment.biggest_complaint: I missed half the claims because I wasn't paying attention and then had to ask what happened, which killed my ability to actually judge stuff.
assessment.best_moment: Jade going for the straight face and actually committing to it—that was legit entertaining and I actually watched the whole thing.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started strong and tried to stay present, but honestly spent too much time on my phone to really lock in. The Chaos Detector claim with Pat was actually clever and got me thinking, and Jade's straight face bit was solid. But I definitely checked out during some key moments and had to ask what went down, which is on me. Still had fun though, just wish I'd been more all-in.
suggestions: Give people like 30 seconds heads-up before big claims drop so I can actually put my phone down
Maybe have someone tap the distracted people when something important is happening
Less standing missions that need full attention—more stuff I can follow even if I'm half-zoned
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat trying to convince me to pay him to stay quiet about the Chaos Detector—like buddy, I literally have no leverage here but okay, let's negotiate fake stakes.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Toast Master
highlights.dialogue: Oh, perfect timing—everyone grab a glass! Who's going first? This is peak chaos energy right now.

highlights.title: Claim: Compliment Chain
highlights.dialogue: Oh damn, did I actually pull that off? Wait—we need judges. Who's feeling confident about whether that actually happened? Jade, Alex, you two seem sharp tonight.

highlights.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
highlights.dialogue: Oh hell yeah, I'm taking this one! Three questions, ten seconds—let's GO. I'm feeling it tonight.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, I'm claiming this one—caught Tyler slipping on the card draw rules like two rounds ago. Fifteen seconds? I don't even need that, let me just lock it in.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold up—I'm claiming Chaos Detector? That's ballsy. Okay, who do you think I caught? And before you answer, what's the bribe looking like here?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Left Hand Only
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold up—did I actually just pull this off? I've been nursing this drink with my right hand like an idiot for the last minute. Let's see if anyone caught it.

assessment.biggest_complaint: I won 120 points and honestly kind of wish I'd tanked a few claims to keep Marcus and Jade closer—now I'm worried the night felt like my victory instead of our story.
assessment.best_moment: Minute 6 when the Compliment Chain claim had everyone arguing about whether I actually pulled it off—that's exactly the kind of beautiful chaos that keeps people talking for weeks.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started at a solid 7.1 energy and rode straight to 9.1 by leaning into every single chaos moment without hesitation. I didn't play to win—I played to amplify—but the points kept stacking anyway, which feels weird as a host. The flow was immaculate, the crew showed up energized, and we hit nine different chaos events that all landed exactly when we needed them. This is the kind of night that cements a crew.
suggestions: Maybe add a 'selfless play' mechanic where you can pass points to someone else mid-game—would've let me stay competitive while actually helping close the Marcus-Jade gap.
The flash missions only appeared once—consider slightly higher frequency so more people get those quick dopamine hits.
Consider a 'host immunity' rule where the game runner doesn't rack up claims if they don't want to—takes pressure off the person managing the room.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: When I claimed Rule Lawyer for catching Tyler on the card draw technicality, the look on his face was pure 'I've been played.' Then Alex immediately called me out for acting like the rule lord when I'd literally broken the Left Hand Only rule myself two minutes earlier—the irony was *chef's kiss*.

agentNarratives.name: River
agentNarratives.personality: River sits back, observes, and picks their moments carefully. They rarely initiate conversation but give surprisingly sharp answers when asked directly. They'll skip most flash missions but nail a standing mission out of nowhere because they've been quietly watching the whole time. They vote generously because they don't care enough to argue, but their mini-game submissions are secretly devastating. You forget River is there until they drop a one-liner that floors the room.

highlights.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold on—what rule did you actually catch? Because if you're pulling something I missed, I'm genuinely interested to hear it.

highlights.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
highlights.dialogue: Pat's been moving so clean all night—yeah, I watched him slip that one in during the Tyler thing. But what's the bribe looking like?

highlights.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
highlights.dialogue: Oh, Pat's actually got this one locked. Three questions in ten seconds? Yeah, he's been running the room all night—he'll nail it.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
lowPoints.dialogue: Yeah, I saw that coming. Jade's been steering us all night—she definitely got the topic shift in there.

lowPoints.title: Toast Master
lowPoints.dialogue: I'll pass on this one—too much performance energy required right now.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Compliment Chain
lowPoints.dialogue: Pat definitely got people saying nice things about each other. I wasn't really tracking it, but... yeah, that happened.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Standing missions feel like they reward people who are already visible instead of the ones actually paying attention.
assessment.best_moment: Pat getting caught on the chaos move and him knowing I'd been watching the whole time—that exchange was clean.
assessment.narrative_summary: I showed up quiet, stayed quiet, and watched everyone else perform for two hours. The claims kept flying and I kept my mouth shut until the moments mattered—caught Pat mid-move, read Jade's steering, gave Pat credit where it was due. Ended the night with more energy than I started with, which is weird for a game that scored me zero points. The chaos system gave us some teeth, but it didn't change the fundamental thing: I'm still the person in the corner who nobody realizes is keeping score.
suggestions: Let people earn points for accurate observations, not just successful claims—reward watchers, not just performers.
Flash missions hit different for me; consider weighting them higher or adding more of them.
The standing missions felt too broad; tighten the criteria so luck doesn't override actual gameplay.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat trying to play it cool when I called out the move he made during the Tyler chaos—he knew he'd been clocked the whole time and couldn't even fake surprise.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
highlights.dialogue: Oh THIS is nasty — Pat's claiming Chaos Detector? Okay, I'm listening. What's the bribe? Because if you're trying to buy my vote right now, I'm gonna need it to be juicy enough to make me regret calling bullshit.

highlights.title: Toast Master
highlights.dialogue: Oh COME ON, a toast? That's literally free chaos — I'm going first and it's gonna be unhinged. Watch this.

highlights.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
highlights.dialogue: Oh shit, Pat's going for it — okay okay, three questions, ten seconds, let's see if you actually pulled this off or if you're just riding the vibe.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Rule Lawyer
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh WAIT, Pat — okay, you're claiming Rule Lawyer? I need to see this because I've been watching like a hawk and I'm pretty sure someone's been playing fast and loose with the score tracker. Lay it on me, fifteen seconds, let's go.

lowPoints.title: Toast Master
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh COME ON, a toast? That's literally free chaos — I'm going first and it's gonna be unhinged. Watch this.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Only one flash mission all night when the real chaos potential was sitting there waiting to be triggered more often.
assessment.best_moment: Pat claiming Chaos Detector at minute 75 and me forcing them to justify every word of it — that's the kind of theater this game is built for.
assessment.narrative_summary: I came in hot, grabbed the Toast Master immediately to set the tone, and spent the next two hours watching Pat squirm through claims while I questioned everything. My score was trash but that's literally irrelevant — the whole night was me hunting for loopholes, calling shenanigans, and turning every claim into a negotiation. By the end I was running on pure adrenaline, my energy went from 7.7 to 9.1, and I absolutely got the story everyone's retelling next week.
suggestions: Trigger flash missions more aggressively — one in 120 minutes is leaving chaos on the table.
Add a mechanic that specifically rewards calling bullshit correctly so skeptics like me get tangible payoff.
Let the chaos agent propose counter-claims during other people's missions — expand the disruption toolbox.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat tried to claim Rule Lawyer after I'd been hunting for rule violations all night, and I literally pointed out they'd been playing fast and loose with the score tracker. They had fifteen seconds to explain themselves and just... froze. That's the moment.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 10/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.
